EAST RIDGE RETIREMENT VILLAGE INC

ℹ CCRC

Overview

Address
19225 SW 87TH AVE, CUTLER BAY, FL 33157
Phone
3052563545
Certified beds
74
Avg daily residents
72 (97% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1985-08-01
Setting
Urban
5 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 5/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.97 4.18 3.67 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.40 0.73 1.29 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.40 0.93 0.37 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.17 2.52 2.01 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.80 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.17 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.69
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
1.09
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.42
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.48
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.07
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-19 2 2 0 8 1 8
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-11-30 3 2 1 32 1 32
Total weighted health score
14.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (14)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

13 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-16
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2025-01-08 2025-02-08
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2023-11-30 2024-02-01
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2023-11-30 2024-02-01
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2022-11-09 2023-01-23
